draft.introduction: The opening oracle of Isaiah establishes the prophetic indictment: Israel has rebelled against the God who raised them. The call to “reason together” offers restoration alongside judgment.

translation.text: Hear, O heavens, and give ear, O earth; for the Lord has spoken: “Children have I reared and brought up, but they have rebelled against me. The ox knows its owner, and the donkey its master’s crib, but Israel does not know, my people do not understand.”
translation.original_text: ἄκουε οὐρανέ καὶ ἐνωτίζου γῆ ὅτι κύριος ἐλάλησεν

translation.text: Wash yourselves; make yourselves clean; remove the evil of your deeds from before my eyes; cease to do evil, learn to do good; seek justice, correct oppression. Come now, let us reason together, says the Lord: though your sins are like scarlet, they shall be as white as snow.

translator_notes.note: “Let us reason together” (δεῦτε καὶ διελεγχθῶμεν) implies a legal disputation. God invites Israel to a courtroom dialogue, establishing forgiveness through argument—a striking image of divine rationality.
